entries.title: Oprah brings her podcast to Amazon's streaming services
entries.summary: Amazon has brought another high-profile podcasting name into its fold after agreeing to a multiyear licensing deal with Oprah Winfrey. Her podcast will expand to two episodes a week starting in July and it will be available across the…
entries.excerpt: Amazon has brought another high-profile podcasting name into its fold after agreeing to a multiyear licensing deal with Oprah Winfrey. Her podcast will expand to two episodes a week starting in July and it will be available across the likes of Prime Video, Amazon Music, Fire TV channels and Audible. New episodes will still hit YouTube and other podcast platforms, according toVariety.

entries.title: Forced Windows updates can now be paused forever
entries.summary: No more getting caught by a forced Windows 11 update while you're in the middle of a meeting or a match. Microsoft announced some major changes coming to Windows Update on its blog , including the ability to indefinitely pause Windows…
entries.excerpt: No more getting caught by a forcedWindows 11update while you're in the middle of a meeting or a match. Microsoft announced some major changes coming to Windows Update onits blog, including the ability to indefinitely pause Windows updates, 35 days at a time.

entries.title: Canadian premier wants to ban social media and AI chatbots for kids in Manitoba
entries.summary: Manitoba could be the first province in Canada to establish a social media ban for kids, but the proposal's details aren't very clear yet. The province's premier, Wab Kinew, announced during a fundraiser event on Saturday and on X that…
entries.excerpt: Manitoba could be the first province in Canada to establish asocial media banfor kids, but the proposal's details aren't very clear yet. The province's premier, Wab Kinew, announced during a fundraiser event on Saturday andon Xthat Manitoba would put in place a ban for social media and AI chatbots for its youth.

entries.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman apologizes for not reporting ChatGPT account of Tumbler Ridge suspect to police
entries.summary: Two months following the deadly shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, OpenAI's Sam Altman has formally apologized for not informing police of the alarming ChatGPT conversations seen with the suspect's account. Before the incident,…
entries.excerpt: Two months following the deadly shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, OpenAI's Sam Altman hasformally apologizedfor not informing police of the alarming ChatGPT conversations seen with the suspect's account. Before the incident, OpenAI banned the account belonging to the alleged shooter, Jesse Van Rootselaar, for violating its usage policy due to potential for real-world violence.

entries.title: Tesla is giving away one year free Supercharging with Model 3 Premium and Performance purchases
entries.summary: Tesla completely ended its free lifetime Supercharging offer way back in 2018, but it has given customers the perk for certain promotions since then. It brought back free Supercharging for Model S and X a couple of times in 2019, for…
entries.excerpt: Tesla completelyendedits free lifetime Supercharging offer way back in 2018, but it has given customers the perk for certain promotions since then. Itbrought backfree Supercharging for Model S and Xa couple of timesin 2019, for instance. The automaker’s latest offer is for new purchases for aModel 3 Premium or Performancevehicle in North America. On its website, Tesla has announced that it’s including one year of free supercharging with a Model 3 Premium or Performance, though the offer is “subject to change or end at any time.”

entries.title: Engadget review recap: DJI Osmo Pocket 4, Recteq X-Fire Pro and Alienware 27 QD-OLED
entries.summary: Engadget’s hottest review roundup truly has it all this week: a new pocket cam, a 2-in-1 smart grill, a pair of drones and a pricey skinny vac. And that’s before we even get to the highly capable gaming display that will only set you back…
entries.excerpt: Engadget’s hottestreviewroundup truly has it all this week: a new pocket cam, a 2-in-1 smart grill, a pair of drones and a pricey skinny vac. And that’s before we even get to the highly capable gaming display that will only set you back $350. Read on to catch up on the reviews you might’ve missed over the last two weeks as we prepare for another slate of big events next month.

entries.title: XChat, the standalone app for messaging on X, is available on iOS now
entries.summary: XChat, the standalone app for accessing X's messaging feature, is available to download now for iOS . X first suggested it would be stripping direct messaging from X in 2025 , but at least for now, XChat is available in the original X app,…
entries.excerpt: XChat, the standalone app for accessing X's messaging feature, is available to download nowfor iOS. X first suggested it would bestripping direct messaging from X in 2025, but at least for now, XChat is available in the original X app, the web and this new app.

entries.title: Maine governor vetoes bill temporarily banning large data centers in the state
entries.summary: The governor of Maine, Janet Mills, has vetoed a bill that halts the construction of large data centers in the state until the fall of 2027. While the bill passed both houses of the Maine's legislature on April 14, and Mills has suggested…
entries.excerpt: The governor of Maine, Janet Mills,has vetoed a billthat halts the construction of large data centers in the state until the fall of 2027. While the billpassed both houses of the Maine's legislatureon April 14, and Millshas suggestedshe'd support a temporary moratorium, the governor wanted a bill that would exemptan existing data center project in Jay, Maine.

entries.title: The DOJ is backing xAI in its lawsuit against Colorado
entries.summary: The Department of Justice has announced that it's intervening on the behalf of xAI in the company's recent lawsuit against the state of Colorado. xAI first filed the suit in early April in response to a recent Colorado law that requires…
entries.excerpt: The Department of Justicehas announcedthat it's intervening on the behalf of xAI in the company's recent lawsuit against the state of Colorado.xAI first filed the suitin early April in response toa recent Colorado lawthat requires developers of "high-risk" AI systems (for example, ones used in healthcare, employment or housing) to both disclose and mitigate the risk of algorithmic discrimination in their systems. The law is set to go into effect in June, and the DOJ is now asking a Colorado District Court to declare it unconstitutional.

entries.title: What you need to know as Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman begins
entries.summary: In a few short days, jury selection will begin in the long-awaited Musk v. Altman case. At the end of that process, an Oakland federal court will task nine regular people with deciding if OpenAI defrauded Elon Musk when it announced, and…
entries.excerpt: In a few short days, jury selection will begin in the long-awaitedMusk v. Altmancase. At the end of that process, an Oakland federal court will task nine regular people with deciding if OpenAI defrauded Elon Musk when it announced, and recently completed, its reorganization to become amore traditional for-profit business. More than just being the venue where two billionaires will air their grievances against one another in public, the trial has the potential to reshape the AI industry.

entries.title: Google plans to invest even more money into Anthropic
entries.summary: Google plans to invest up to $40 billion into Anthropic in what could be viewed as a circular deal with the AI startup (and frequent competitor), Bloomberg reports . The search giant has invested in Anthropic at multiple points in the…
entries.excerpt: Google plans to invest up to $40 billion into Anthropic in what could be viewed as a circular deal with the AI startup (and frequent competitor),Bloombergreports. The search gianthas invested in Anthropicat multiple points in the past, but this new investment comes after an announcement that the AI startup hadsigned a joint agreement with Google and Broadcomfor "multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity."
